components.text: An old hermit by the river did not teach me. He pointed at the water and said nothing for several minutes, then left. I sat there for what I think was two days.

At some point — cold, hungry, irritated — something shifted inside my chest. Not my heart. Something behind it.
I ran after the hermit. He was already gone. I have been chasing that feeling ever since.

What I learned after much pain: Qi does not respond to wanting. It responds to a stillness that has given up on wanting. I discovered this by almost starving on a riverbank.
Be still. Be patient. The moment you stop performing patience and actually become it, something answers.
Do not skip this part. Everything else depends on it.
